title: A worker for an international organization needs a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## You need a work permit A worker for an international organization needs a work permit
If you’re not accredited and are working for an international organization, you need a work permit to work in Canada.
You can apply for an employer-specific work permit (opens in a new tab) .
If the international organization is recognized under Foreign Missions and International Organizations Act , your employer doesn’t need to get a labour market impact assessment or to submit an offer of employment to hire you.
Examples of these organizations include:
- the International Civil Aviation Organization
- the Secretariat of the Convention on Biological Diversity See Global Affairs Canada’s complete list of international organizations .
- See Global Affairs Canada’s complete list of international organizations .
- an example of this category of work would be interns working at international organizations in Canada

## When you complete your application
You must provide a copy of your job contract or employment letter when you apply.
When you fill out your work permit application form, select “Other” as the type of work permit you’re applying for. For more details, see the instruction guide that applies to you:
- Workers outside Canada
How to apply for a work permit

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
